chunks.title: Job's Introduction and Reaction
chunks.reason: Job introduces his speech and expresses his dismay at the thought of speaking.

chunks.title: The Prosperity of the Wicked
chunks.reason: Job describes how the wicked live in comfort and power despite their actions.

chunks.title: The Fate of the Wicked and the Righteous
chunks.reason: Job questions if disaster always comes to the wicked and notes that both die regardless of their life.

chunks.title: Refutation of Friends' Views
chunks.reason: Job counters his friends' specific claims about the wicked's end and their families.

chunks.title: Conclusion of Job's Speech
chunks.reason: Job concludes by challenging the validity of his friends' consolation.
